---
title: "Timely and Effective Care Across U.S. States"
subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2024 - Final Project"
author:
  - name: "Christopher Paskins"
    affiliations:
      - name: "School of Information, University of Arizona"
description: "A state-level look at emergency-department throughput and treatment-effectiveness using CMS Timely and Effective Care data."
format:
   html:
    code-tools: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
execute:
  warning: false
  echo: false
---

## Abstract

This project examines hospital quality at the U.S. state level using the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) "Timely and Effective Care" dataset, curated for TidyTuesday (week 14, 2025). The data cover 22 measures across 56 jurisdictions - 50 states, the District of Columbia, and U.S. territories for 2023–2024 reporting periods, recorded in a long/tidy layout of one row per state and measure. To enable richer comparisons, we derive a four-level U.S. Census region from each state code and merge in 2024 state population estimates.

We pursue two distinct questions. The first asks how the median time patients spend in the emergency department before leaving varies by ED patient-volume tier and Census region, and whether it tracks state population. The second asks how states compare across treatment-effectiveness measures, stroke CT timeliness, colonoscopy follow-up appropriateness, safe opioid use, and whether strong performance on one measure tends to accompany strong performance on the others.

Our approach pivots the relevant measures into a tidy, state-level frame for each question, joins the derived region and population variables, and visualizes the patterns with `ggplot2`. Because the data are observational, all findings are framed as associations rather than causal effects.
